nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Mesoscopic solar cell fabricated by sequential deposition: PbI2 first introduced from DMF solution (~1 M, 70C) into 350 nm mesoporous TiO2, then transformed to CH3NH3PbI3 by dipping in CH3NH3I/2-propanol (10 mg/ml) for 20 seconds, with spiro-MeOTAD HTM doped with Co(III) complex (10 mol%), measured under AM 1.5G

nodes.label: sequential_method
nodes.content: Two-step deposition method: PbI2 infiltrated into mesoporous TiO2 (pore size ~22 nm confines PbI2 crystals), then converted to perovskite by CH3NH3I solution. The layered PbI2 structure (I-Pb-I planes with weak interlayer van der Waals bonding) facilitates cation insertion

nodes.label: pce_15_pct
nodes.content: Best-performing sequential-deposition cell achieves PCE of 15.0% with Jsc=20.0 mA/cm2, Voc=0.993 V, and fill factor 0.73, measured at 96.4 mW/cm2

nodes.label: pce_certified_14_14
nodes.content: An accredited photovoltaic calibration laboratory certifies PCE of 14.14% under standard AM 1.5G reporting conditions, confirming the validity of the lab measurements

nodes.label: fast_conversion
nodes.content: Conversion of nanoconfined PbI2 to CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite is complete within seconds of exposure to CH3NH3I solution, as shown by immediate disappearance of PbI2 XRD (001) peak and appearance of tetragonal perovskite reflections

nodes.label: nanoconfinement_enhances_kinetics
nodes.content: Confining PbI2 crystals to ~22 nm within mesoporous TiO2 drastically enhances their rate of conversion to perovskite compared to bulk PbI2 films (50-200 nm crystallites), where conversion remains incomplete even after 45 minutes

nodes.label: apce_near_unity
nodes.content: The absorbed-photon-to-current conversion efficiency (APCE) is greater than 90% over the whole visible region without correction for reflective losses, indicating near-unity quantum yield for charge carrier generation and collection

nodes.label: ipce_peak_90_pct
nodes.content: IPCE reaches peak values exceeding 90% in the short-wavelength visible region, with photocurrent generation starting at 800 nm consistent with the CH3NH3PbI3 bandgap

nodes.label: typical_device_12_9
nodes.content: Typical device shows PCE of 12.9% with Jsc=17.1 mA/cm2, Voc=0.992 V, and fill factor 0.73 at 95.6 mW/cm2; batch average 12.0% +/- 0.5% (n=10)

nodes.label: high_reproducibility
nodes.content: Sequential deposition greatly increases reproducibility with standard deviation of only 0.5% PCE across 10 devices, compared to large morphological variations and performance spread in single-step deposition

nodes.label: stability_500h
nodes.content: Sealed cell maintained under constant light soaking (~100 mW/cm2, 45C) with maximum-power-point tracking retains >80% of initial PCE after 500 hours, with no change in short-circuit photocurrent

nodes.label: no_photodegradation
nodes.content: No photodegradation of the perovskite light harvester is observed during 500 hours of light soaking; the PCE decrease is solely due to Voc and fill factor decline from reduced shunt resistance

nodes.label: co_dopant_htm
nodes.content: Using a Co(III) complex as p-type dopant for spiro-MeOTAD at 10 mol% doping level ensures sufficient conductivity and low series resistance in the HTM

nodes.label: prewetting_improves
nodes.content: A pre-wetting step (dipping in 2-propanol for 1-2 s before CH3NH3I solution) locally decreases CH3NH3I concentration, inducing growth of larger perovskite crystals and increased light scattering for improved long-wavelength response

nodes.label: degradation_mechanism
nodes.content: What is the precise mechanism causing the decrease in shunt resistance during 500-hour light soaking, and can it be eliminated through interface engineering?
Single-step perovskite deposition from mixed PbX2/CH3NH3X solution produces large morphological variations and wide spread of photovoltaic performance

nodes.label: htmc_free_config

nodes.label: two_d_three_d_interface
nodes.content: Engineering a 2D/3D (HOOC(CH2)4NH3)2PbI4/CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite junction creates an exceptional gradually-organized multi-dimensional interface that combines 2D perovskite stability with 3D perovskite charge transport

nodes.label: one_year_stability
nodes.content: 2D/3D engineered perovskite devices maintain stable performance for over one year, corresponding to >10,000 hours of operation

nodes.label: carbon_cell_12_9_pct
nodes.content: 2D/3D perovskite achieves up to 12.9% efficiency in carbon-based architecture and 14.6% in standard mesoporous solar cells

nodes.label: module_10x10_stable
nodes.content: 10x10 cm2 solar modules fabricated by fully printable industrial-scale process deliver 11.2% efficiency stable for >10,000 hours with zero loss in performance measured under controlled standard conditions

nodes.label: two_d_water_resistant
nodes.content: 2D perovskites exhibit superior stability and water resistance compared to 3D perovskites, preventing hydrolysis and irreversible degradation under moisture

nodes.label: gradual_interface
nodes.content: The 2D/3D interface forms a gradually-organized multi-dimensional structure that provides continuous charge transport pathways while maintaining enhanced stability

nodes.label: scalable_printable
nodes.content: The 2D/3D perovskite architecture is compatible with fully printable industrial-scale processes for large-area module fabrication

nodes.label: oxygen_moisture_tolerant
nodes.content: 2D/3D perovskite modules maintain stable performance under controlled standard conditions in the presence of oxygen and moisture

nodes.label: market_requirements_approach
nodes.content: Achieving >10,000 hours stability approaches the market requirement of <10% drop in PCE for at least 1,000 hours on standard accelerated aging tests

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Bifacial perovskite minimodules using p-i-n architecture with PTAA/C60 as HTL/ETL. Perovskite compositions MA0.7FA0.3PbI3 and FA0.92Cs0.08PbI3. Aperture areas of 14.3-25.1 cm2. ITO rear electrode with Ag grids, ALD SnO2 buffer layer. Blade-coated at room temperature in ambient air.

nodes.label: bifacial_advantage
nodes.content: Bifacial modules harvest reflected and diffused sunlight from the rear side, gaining 5-30% more power than monofacial modules depending on albedo. Average albedo of 0.2 or higher is common in many locations.

nodes.label: bifacial_front_20
nodes.title: bifacial_minimodule_20_front
nodes.content: Bifacial perovskite minimodules achieve a front aperture efficiency of 20.2% and rear aperture efficiency of 15.0%, with NREL-certified stabilized front efficiency of 19.2% and rear efficiency of 14.1%

nodes.label: power_generation_density
nodes.title: bifacial_pgd_23_mw
nodes.content: Bifacial minimodules achieve a power-generation density of over 23 mW cm-2 at albedo of 0.2, higher than the best certified monofacial modules, and up to 24.7 mW cm-2 at albedo of 0.3

nodes.label: small_cell_264_pgd
nodes.title: small_cell_264_pgd
nodes.content: Small-area single-junction bifacial PSCs achieve a power-generation density of 26.4 mW cm-2 under 1 sun and albedo of 0.2

nodes.label: light_soaking_6000h
nodes.title: light_soaking_6000h_t97
nodes.content: Bifacial minimodules retain 97% of initial PCE after over 6000 hours of light soaking under 1 sun at 60+/-5 C, the most stable reported perovskite minimodule at the time of publication

nodes.label: damp_heat_1000h
nodes.title: damp_heat_1000h_84_percent
nodes.content: Bifacial minimodules maintain approximately 84% of initial efficiency after damp-heat testing for over 1000 hours (85 C, 85% RH)

nodes.label: tpfb_moisture_protection
nodes.title: tpfb_moisture_protection
nodes.content: Adding hydrophobic tris(pentafluorophenyl)borane (TPFB) to the PTAA hole transport layer spreads to the perovskite surface and significantly enhances moisture resistance during ALD processing, improving device reproducibility for large-area modules

nodes.label: sio2_np_scattering
nodes.title: sio2_np_light_scattering
nodes.content: Embedding 500 nm SiO2 nanoparticles in perovskite films recovers absorption loss from the absent reflective metal electrode by scattering red and NIR light, increasing Jsc by 0.8 mA cm-2 without introducing additional non-radiative recombination

nodes.label: ag_grid_optimization
nodes.title: ag_grid_optimization
nodes.content: Optimally spaced silver grids (2 mm spacing) on the rear ITO electrode reduce resistance loss from 8.6% to less than 0.9% while maintaining bifaciality of 74.3%

nodes.label: ald_sno2_stability
nodes.title: ald_sno2_module_stability
nodes.content: ALD SnO2 buffer layer greatly reduces perovskite damage during laser scribing and stabilizes the C60/BCP interface by replacing the thermally unstable BCP layer

nodes.label: bifaciality_74
nodes.title: bifaciality_74_percent
nodes.content: Bifacial minimodules achieve a bifaciality of 74.3% and gain 15% more power output at albedo of 0.2 compared with monofacial modules

nodes.label: module_reproducibility
nodes.title: module_reproducibility
nodes.content: Across eight bifacial minimodules with Ag grids, average front and rear aperture efficiencies reach 19.5% and 14.5% respectively, demonstrating good fabrication reproducibility

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells on silicon heterojunction bottom cells. Perovskite bandgap 1.69 eV. Device stack: IZO/HTL201/perovskite/LiF/EDAI/C60/SnO2/IZO/Ag/MgF2. Device area 1.004 cm2. Certified by European Solar Test Installation (ESTI).

nodes.label: sam_challenge
nodes.content: Self-assembled monolayers (SAMs) serve as hole-selective layers in inverted PSCs. Symmetric SAMs like Me-4PACz and MeO-4PACz have limited coverage on TCO substrates and non-optimal interaction with perovskites, constraining tandem device performance.

nodes.label: certified_34_58
nodes.title: certified_34_58_percent
nodes.content: Perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells using asymmetric SAM HTL201 achieve a certified PCE of 34.58% (ESTI certification) with Voc of nearly 2.00 V, representing a record efficiency for perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells

nodes.label: champion_34_60
nodes.title: champion_34_60_percent
nodes.content: The champion tandem device achieves an in-house PCE of 34.60% with Voc of 2.001 V, Jsc of 20.64 mA cm-2, and FF of 83.79%

nodes.label: asymmetric_sam_htl201
nodes.title: asymmetric_sam_htl201
nodes.content: An asymmetric self-assembled monolayer (HTL201) featuring an anchoring group and spacer flanking a carbazole core shows minimized steric hindrance and improved coverage on IZO recombination layer compared to symmetric SAMs (Me-4PACz, MeO-4PACz)

nodes.label: enhanced_coverage
nodes.title: enhanced_sam_coverage
nodes.content: HTL201 forms denser monolayer coverage on IZO substrates than Me-4PACz and MeO-4PACz, with higher coverage factor confirmed by XPS and molecular dynamics simulations showing stronger adsorption energy

nodes.label: buried_interface_passivation
nodes.title: buried_interface_passivation
nodes.content: Strong coordination interaction between HTL201 and perovskite film effectively reduces non-radiative recombination at the buried interface, achieving PLQY of 0.399% (versus 0.346% for Me-4PACz) and QFLS of 1.270 V on textured silicon substrates

nodes.label: voc_near_2v
nodes.title: voc_near_2_volts
nodes.content: The optimized energy-level alignment between perovskite and HTL201 enables Voc approaching 2.00 V for perovskite/silicon tandems, among the highest reported voltages for this architecture

nodes.label: carrier_lifetime_5860ns
nodes.title: carrier_lifetime_5860_ns
nodes.content: Perovskite films on HTL201 show carrier lifetime of 5,860 ns versus 5,574 ns on Me-4PACz and 1,813 ns on MeO-4PACz, indicating lower trap density at the buried interface

nodes.label: avg_pce_34_22
nodes.title: avg_pce_34_22_percent
nodes.content: Devices using HTL201 achieve an average PCE of 34.22% across 20 devices, compared with 32.18% for Me-4PACz and 33.34% for MeO-4PACz

nodes.label: asymmetric_design_general
nodes.title: asymmetric_design_general
nodes.content: The asymmetric molecular design strategy is generally successful: HTL201-like derivatives HTL207 (n=2) and HTL203 (n=4) also deliver higher efficiency than Me-4PACz and MeO-4PACz, with HTL201 being optimal

nodes.label: delayed_crystallization
nodes.title: delayed_crystallization
nodes.content: HTL201-coated substrates delay perovskite nucleation and crystallization (nucleation at 540 s vs 180 s for Me-4PACz), effectively improving perovskite film quality and (100) plane orientation

nodes.label: mppt_stability_25c
nodes.title: mppt_stability_25c
nodes.content: Encapsulated HTL201-based tandem devices show stable MPP tracking at 25 C under continuous 1-sun illumination, demonstrating promising operational stability

nodes.label: mppt_stability_45c
nodes.title: mppt_stability_45c
nodes.content: Encapsulated HTL201-based tandem devices show degraded but still functional MPP tracking at 45 C, indicating thermal sensitivity of perovskite/Si tandems requires further improvement

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Two-terminal monolithic perovskite/silicon tandem solar cells built on double-side-textured Czochralski-based silicon heterojunction cells. Perovskite bandgap 1.69 eV. Inverted p-i-n configuration. Device area 1.0 cm2. NREL-certified performance.

nodes.label: asymmetric_texture
nodes.content: The silicon bottom cell uses asymmetrically sized textures: mildly textured front surface (0.5-1 um pyramids) for solution-processed perovskite coverage, and heavily textured rear surface (>3 um pyramids) for uncompromised rear passivation and improved infrared response.

nodes.label: certified_33_89
nodes.title: certified_33_89_percent
nodes.content: The perovskite/silicon tandem achieves an independently certified stabilized PCE of 33.89% with FF of 83.0% and Voc of nearly 1.97 V, the first double-junction tandem exceeding the single-junction Shockley-Queisser limit of 33.7%

nodes.label: champion_34_1
nodes.title: champion_pce_34_1_percent
nodes.content: The champion tandem cell achieves an in-house PCE of 34.1% with Jsc of 20.68 mA cm-2, Voc of 1.980 V, and FF of 83.2%

nodes.label: bilayer_passivation
nodes.title: bilayer_lif_edai_passivation
nodes.content: A bilayer interface passivation strategy combining nanoscale discretely distributed LiF ultrathin layer with diammonium diiodide (EDAI) molecule achieves optimal trade-off between passivation and charge extraction at the perovskite/C60 interface

nodes.label: edai_chemical_passivation
nodes.title: edai_chemical_passivation
nodes.content: EDAI molecules chemically passivate uncoordinated Pb2+ surface defects by forming coordinate bonds, eliminating metallic Pb(0) peaks observed in XPS, while LiF alone does not provide this chemical passivation

nodes.label: intertwined_structure
nodes.title: intertwined_nanocale_contacts
nodes.content: The LiF and EDAI layers are intertwined at the nanoscale: the discontinuous LiF layer allows EDAI to locally contact the perovskite surface, creating nanoscale localized contacts that balance electron tunnelling (through LiF) with direct transport (through EDAI/C60)

nodes.label: asymmetric_texture_benefit
nodes.title: asymmetric_texture_benefit
nodes.content: The asymmetrically textured silicon wafer (small front pyramids, large rear pyramids) preserves minority carrier lifetime of 3.4 ms (comparable to 3.2 ms for standard double-sided texture) and improves infrared EQE response compared with double-sided mild texture

nodes.label: voc_progression
nodes.title: voc_ff_progression_passivation
nodes.content: The bilayer passivation improves average Voc from below 1.90 V (unpassivated) to 1.96 V (LiF/EDAI), and FF from 76.2% to 80.8% for single-junction perovskite devices

nodes.label: storage_stability
nodes.title: storage_stability_90_percent_53d
nodes.content: Devices with LiF/EDAI bilayer passivation retain approximately 90% of original PCE after 53 days of air storage, compared with 82% for LiF-treated control devices

nodes.label: mppt_stability_1200h
nodes.title: mppt_stability_1200h
nodes.content: The bilayer-treated tandem retains approximately 80% of initial PCE after 1200 hours of MPP tracking under simulated 1-sun at room temperature, compared with less than 60% for LiF-treated control

nodes.label: average_pce_33
nodes.title: average_pce_exceeding_33
nodes.content: The bilayer-treated tandem devices achieve an average PCE exceeding 33% with some devices reaching above 33.8%, demonstrating excellent reproducibility at 1 cm2 scale

nodes.label: sq_limit_breakthrough
nodes.title: sq_limit_breakthrough
nodes.content: This work represents the first reported certified efficiency of a two-junction tandem solar cell exceeding the single-junction Shockley-Queisser limit of 33.7%, confirming the fundamental advantage of multi-junction architectures

nodes.label: dft_binding_energy
nodes.title: dft_binding_energy_eda
nodes.content: DFT calculations show EDA2+ has binding energies of -6.6 and -8.4 eV on FAI-rich and PbI2-rich FAPbI3 surfaces respectively, substantially larger than monoammonium PA+, and adopts a horizontal bridge-like configuration that maximizes out-of-plane charge transport

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Entirely roll-to-roll fabricated perovskite solar cell modules on flexible PET substrates. Device stack: flexible TCE/SnO2/FA0.45MA0.55PbI3/HTAB-P3HT/carbon electrode with screen-printed Ag grids. All layers deposited by industrial R2R tools (slot-die, reverse gravure, screen printing) under ambient room conditions.

nodes.label: r2r_challenges
nodes.content: Translating lab-scale perovskite solar cells to R2R manufacturing requires replacing vacuum-deposited electrodes with printable alternatives, developing ambient-compatible processing, and controlling crystallization on continuously moving flexible substrates.

nodes.label: first_r2r_modules
nodes.title: first_r2r_modules
nodes.content: This work reports the first demonstration of perovskite solar cell modules, comprising serially-interconnected cells, produced entirely using industrial roll-to-roll printing tools under ambient room conditions

nodes.label: r2r_cell_15_5
nodes.title: r2r_cell_15_5_percent
nodes.content: Fully R2R-printed individual perovskite solar cells achieve a record PCE of 15.5% with Jsc of 19.9 mA cm-2, FF of 76.1%, and Voc of 1.02 V, using a printed carbon electrode to replace vacuum-deposited gold

nodes.label: r2r_module_11
nodes.title: r2r_module_11_percent
nodes.content: Entirely R2R-fabricated modules (49.5 cm2 active area, 5 cells in series) achieve up to 11.0% active-area PCE with 192 mA current, 62.3% FF, and 4.59 V Voc

nodes.label: carbon_electrode
nodes.title: carbon_electrode_r2r
nodes.content: Perovskite-friendly carbon inks enable vacuum-free printed back electrodes compatible with R2R manufacturing, replacing costly vacuum-deposited Au electrodes

nodes.label: pfsd_technique
nodes.title: pfsd_edge_blowing
nodes.content: The printing-friendly sequential deposition (PFSD) technique with shallow-angle edge blowing produces high-quality perovskite films on continuously moving substrates under ambient conditions (40-50% RH), with mirror-like film quality and no PbI2 residue

nodes.label: htab_p3ht_htl
nodes.title: htab_p3ht_htl
nodes.content: The HTAB-P3HT hole transport layer system outperforms PPDT2FBT, achieving higher PCE and narrower performance distribution in R2R fabrication, by passivating perovskite surface traps and promoting self-assembly of P3HT

nodes.label: high_throughput_screening
nodes.title: high_throughput_screening
nodes.content: An automated R2R platform enables fabrication and testing of over 10,000 solar cells per day, with 1600 cells fabricated in a single experiment using 20 parameter combinations for rapid optimization

nodes.label: humidity_tolerance
nodes.title: humidity_tolerance_r2r
nodes.content: Reliable production of R2R-fabricated PSCs with average PCE of ~13% is confirmed regardless of ambient humidity (30-60% RH), demonstrating manufacturing robustness

nodes.label: cost_prediction
nodes.title: cost_prediction_0_7_usd
nodes.content: A cost model predicts manufacturing cost of ~0.7 USD W-1 for a production rate of 1,000,000 m2 per year in Australia, with potential for further cost reduction below 0.5 USD W-1 by eliminating remaining high-cost components

nodes.label: geometric_fill_factor
nodes.title: geometric_fill_factor_75
nodes.content: R2R-printed modules achieve a geometric fill factor (GFF) of 75% using stripe-pattern interconnection, lower than laser-scribed modules (99%) but compatible with high-throughput manufacturing

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Bilayer architecture perovskite solar cell: glass/FTO/bl-TiO2/mp-TiO2-perovskite nanocomposite/perovskite upper layer (100-300 nm)/PTAA/Au. Uses MAPb(I1-xBrx)3 (x=0.1-0.15) as absorber and poly(triarylamine) (PTAA) as hole-transporting material, fully solution-processed

nodes.label: solvent_engineering
nodes.content: Perovskite deposited by spin-coating from GBL+DMSO mixed solvent followed by toluene drop-casting during spinning. The toluene rapidly removes excess DMSO and freezes constituents into a uniform MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase, which converts to perovskite upon annealing at 100C for 10 min

nodes.label: mixed_halide_composition
nodes.content: MAPb(I1-xBrx)3 with x=0.1-0.15 used because 10-15 mol% Br substitution for I greatly improves stability in ambient atmosphere while maintaining similar photovoltaic performance over the compositional range

nodes.label: pce_certified_16_2
nodes.content: Fully solution-processed perovskite solar cell achieves certified PCE of 16.2% under standard reporting conditions with no hysteresis, using MAPb(I0.85Br0.15)3 and PTAA hole transporter

nodes.label: intermediate_phase
nodes.content: The MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase is a new crystalline compound (confirmed by XRD peaks at 6.55, 7.21, and 9.17 degrees and FTIR showing both N-H and S-O deformation modes) that converts to perovskite upon annealing at 130C

nodes.label: toluene_critical
nodes.content: Without toluene drop-casting, the film adopts a textile-like inhomogeneous layer that does not fully cover the substrate. The toluene drip is critically important for producing uniform, dense, and fully covering perovskite films

nodes.label: rms_roughness_6nm
nodes.content: The intermediate phase film has RMS roughness of 6.0 nm and the resulting perovskite film has RMS roughness of 8.3 nm (AFM over 3x3 um2), demonstrating extremely smooth and uniform surface morphology

nodes.label: bilayer_architecture
nodes.content: The bilayer architecture combining mesoscopic mp-TiO2-perovskite nanocomposite base with a planar perovskite upper layer (100-300 nm) effectively absorbs light and collects charges, merging advantages of mesoscopic and planar structures

nodes.label: dmpso_retards_reaction
nodes.content: DMSO in the MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase retards the rapid reaction between PbI2 and MAI during solvent evaporation, enabling controlled crystallization and uniform film formation

nodes.label: br_improves_ambient_stability
nodes.content: Substituting 10-15 mol% Br- for I- in MAPbI3 greatly improves stability in ambient atmosphere while the dual halide material demonstrates similar photovoltaic performance over the compositional range

nodes.label: conversion_at_130c
nodes.content: The MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase coexists with perovskite at 100C and completely converts to perovskite at 130C, as monitored by in situ high-temperature XRD

nodes.label: ptaa_htm
nodes.content: Poly(triarylamine) (PTAA) used as hole-transporting material achieves high performance in combination with the bilayer perovskite architecture, enabling the certified 16.2% PCE

nodes.label: no_hysteresis
nodes.content: The solvent-engineered perovskite solar cells show no hysteresis in J-V measurements, indicating balanced charge transport and minimal ion migration effects in the bilayer architecture

nodes.label: gbl_only_inhomogeneous
nodes.content: In pure GBL solvent (without DMSO), perovskite crystals form immediately during spin-coating, producing inhomogeneous islands with low surface coverage regardless of toluene drip treatment

nodes.label: intermediate_mechanism
nodes.content: What is the precise atomic structure of the MAI-PbI2-DMSO intermediate phase, and how does the guest molecule arrangement control the subsequent perovskite crystallization?

nodes.content: Bilayer solar-cell architecture with perovskite-infiltrated mesoporous TiO2 electrodes and upper perovskite layer obtained by solvent engineering techniques

nodes.label: fa_narrower_bandgap
nodes.content: FAPbI3 has a bandgap of 1.48 eV with absorption edge of 840 nm, narrower than MAPbI3 (1.5-1.6 eV, edge ~800 nm), enabling broader solar spectrum absorption

nodes.label: fa_phase_unstable
nodes.content: Pure FAPbI3 black perovskite phase (alpha-phase) is thermally unstable, converting to yellow delta-phase at room temperature in humid atmosphere

nodes.label: mapbbr3_stabilizes_fapbi3
nodes.content: Incorporation of MAPbBr3 into FAPbI3 stabilizes the perovskite phase of FAPbI3 and prevents conversion to the non-perovskite delta-phase

nodes.label: optimal_composition_x15
nodes.content: Optimal composition (FAPbI3)0.85(MAPbBr3)0.15 achieves maximum Jsc of 22 mA/cm2, Voc of 1.08 V, fill factor 73%, and PCE >18% under standard AM1.5G illumination

nodes.label: negligible_hysteresis
nodes.content: FAPbI3/MAPbBr3 mixed perovskite solar cells show negligible hysteresis in J-V curves compared to MAPbI3-based cells, due to better balance of electron and hole transport

nodes.label: pce_over_18_pct
nodes.content: The (FAPbI3)0.85(MAPbBr3)0.15 composition achieves stabilized power conversion efficiency exceeding 18% under standard illumination of 100 mW/cm2

nodes.label: hole_diffusion_superior
nodes.content: FAPbI3 has a hole-diffusion length (~813 nm) 4.6 times longer than its electron-diffusion length (~177 nm), contrasting with MAPbI3 where electron diffusion length exceeds hole diffusion length

nodes.label: bandgap_tunable
nodes.content: The bandgap of (FAPbI3)1-x(MAPbBr3)x is tunable by adjusting the molar ratio x, with absorption edge shifting from 840 nm (x=0) to shorter wavelengths as x increases

nodes.label: carrier_balance_improved
nodes.content: Mixing FAPbI3 with MAPbBr3 improves the balance between electron and hole transport, which is critical for high-efficiency perovskite solar cells

nodes.label: thermal_stability_improved
nodes.content: The mixed FAPbI3/MAPbBr3 composition shows improved thermal stability compared to pure FAPbI3, with the perovskite phase remaining stable at 100C annealing temperature

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: FAPbI3 perovskite solar cells fabricated on mesoporous TiO2/FTO substrates using anti-solvent method with 2 mol% formamidinium formate (FAHCOO) added to the FAPbI3 precursor solution, 35 mol% MACl additive, Spiro-OMeTAD hole transport layer, Au electrode, measured under AM 1.5G at 100 mW/cm2

nodes.label: formate_passivation
nodes.content: Pseudo-halide formate anion (HCOO-) introduced via formamidinium formate (FAHCOO) into FAPbI3 precursor at 1-4 mol%; formate does not substitute iodide in the perovskite lattice but passivates surface iodide vacancy defects at grain boundaries

nodes.label: pce_25_6_pct
nodes.content: FAPbI3 PSC with 2% formate additive achieves maximum PCE of 25.59% with Jsc=26.35 mA/cm2, Voc=1.189 V, and fill factor 81.7%, under reverse scan

nodes.label: certified_25_2_pct
nodes.content: Target FAPbI3 PSC certified at Newport with quasi-steady-state PCE of 25.21%, Voc=1.174 V, Jsc=26.25 mA/cm2, fill factor 81.8%

nodes.label: voc_96_pct_sql
nodes.content: The Voc of 1.21 V achieved in the formate-treated cell represents 96% of the Shockley-Queisser limit of 1.25 V for FAPbI3, the highest Voc ratio yet obtained

nodes.label: fivefold_el_reduction
nodes.content: Formate treatment results in a fivefold reduction in non-radiative recombination rate, with EQE_EL increasing from 2.2% (reference) to 10.1% (target) at injection current density corresponding to 1-sun Jsc

nodes.label: ideality_factor_reduction
nodes.content: The ideality factor (n_id) of the formate-treated cell is 1.18, lower than the reference cell value of 1.52 and the previously reported best of 1.27, indicating reduced trap-assisted recombination

nodes.label: formate_not_in_lattice
nodes.content: Solid-state NMR (207Pb and 13C) measurements confirm that HCOO- anions do not substitute for iodide in the alpha-FAPbI3 lattice; the 207Pb resonance remains unchanged at 1543 ppm even with 5% FAHCOO addition

nodes.label: formate_passivates_vacancies
nodes.content: HCOO- anions passivate iodide vacancy defects at grain surfaces and boundaries by binding to undercoordinated Pb2+ sites, with the highest binding energy among all tested anions (Cl-, Br-, I-, BF4-) at I-vacant sites

nodes.label: formate_enhances_crystallinity
nodes.content: Adding 2% FAHCOO strongly enhances the crystallinity of alpha-FAPbI3 films, eliminating the delta-phase under high humidity (100% RH, 30C) and reducing XRD peak full-width at half-maximum

nodes.label: shelf_life_improvement
nodes.content: Unencapsulated formate-treated PSCs show only 10% PCE degradation after 1000 h shelf-life storage in the dark at 25C and 20% RH, compared to 35% degradation for the reference cell

nodes.label: thermal_stability_improvement
nodes.content: Target PSC retains approximately 80% of initial efficiency after 1000 h annealing at 60C under 20% RH, compared to only 40% for the reference cell

nodes.label: operational_stability_improvement
nodes.content: Under continuous 1-sun MPP tracking in N2 atmosphere, the target cell loses only ~15% of initial efficiency over 450 h while the reference cell loses ~30%, measured at approximately 35C device temperature without active cooling

nodes.label: carrier_lifetime_increase
nodes.content: Formate-treated FAPbI3 films show significantly longer charge-carrier recombination lifetimes as measured by time-resolved photoluminescence, consistent with reduced trap-mediated non-radiative recombination confirmed by EQE_EL and SCLC

nodes.label: md_simulation_formate_binding
nodes.content: Ab initio molecular dynamics simulations show HCOO- coordinates strongly with Pb2+ in precursor solution, slowing crystal growth to produce larger stacked grains; HCOO- forms hydrogen-bonded networks with FA+ at the perovskite surface

nodes.label: optimal_formate_conc
nodes.content: What is the optimal FAHCOO concentration for balancing passivation effectiveness against potential adverse effects at higher concentrations, and does this optimum transfer to other perovskite compositions?

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: All-solid-state mesoscopic heterojunction solar cell with CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite nanoparticles deposited on 0.6 um thick mesoporous TiO2, pores infiltrated with spiro-MeOTAD hole conductor, measured under AM 1.5G solar illumination at 100 mW/cm2

nodes.label: solid_state_device
nodes.content: Device structure: FTO / compact TiO2 / mesoporous TiO2 (0.6 um) infiltrated with CH3NH3PbI3 / spiro-MeOTAD / Au, fabricated without liquid electrolyte

nodes.label: pce_9_7_pct
nodes.content: Solid-state CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite cell achieves PCE of 9.7% with Jsc=17.6 mA/cm2, Voc=0.888 V, and fill factor 0.62 under AM 1.5G illumination, the highest reported for perovskite-sensitized cells at the time

nodes.label: high_absorption_coefficient
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite nanocrystals exhibit an absorption coefficient of 1.5x10^4 cm-1 at 550 nm, approximately one order of magnitude higher than conventional N719 dye

nodes.label: optical_bandgap_direct
nodes.content: The optical band gap of CH3NH3PbI3 on TiO2 is 1.5 eV determined by Kubelka-Munk analysis, and the absorption occurs via a direct allowed transition

nodes.label: band_alignment
nodes.content: Valence band of CH3NH3PbI3 is at -5.43 eV below vacuum and conduction band at -3.93 eV, which is slightly higher than the TiO2 conduction band, providing well-aligned band positions for charge separation

nodes.label: hole_transfer_mechanism
nodes.content: Charge separation proceeds via hole injection from excited CH3NH3PbI3 into spiro-MeOTAD, confirmed by photo-induced absorption (PIA) showing oxidized spiro-MeOTAD signature at 1340 nm

nodes.label: submicron_sufficient
nodes.content: Submicron-thick (0.6 um) mesoporous TiO2 film can deliver over 9% PCE due to the large optical absorption cross-section of perovskite nanoparticles and complete pore filling by the hole conductor

nodes.label: ipce_plateau
nodes.content: IPCE reaches a broad maximum at 450 nm and remains above 50% up to 750 nm, indicating efficient photon harvesting by CH3NH3PbI3 nanoparticles in the 0.6 um mesoporous TiO2 film

nodes.label: linear_photocurrent
nodes.content: Photocurrent density is linearly proportional to light intensity, indicating a non-space-charge-limited structure with little difference between electron and hole mobility at the TiO2/perovskite/spiro-MeOTAD junction

nodes.label: voc_thickness_dependence
nodes.content: Open-circuit voltage decreases from ~0.9 V to ~0.8 V as TiO2 film thickness increases from 0.6 um to 1.5 um, attributed to increased dark current that lowers electron concentration and quasi-Fermi level

nodes.label: reductive_quenching
nodes.content: Femtosecond transient absorption confirms rapid reductive quenching of excited CH3NH3PbI3 by spiro-MeOTAD, while no significant electron injection from perovskite into TiO2 is observed on the sub-ns timescale

nodes.label: solid_state_stability
nodes.content: Solid-state perovskite cell shows excellent long-term stability over 500 hours without encapsulation in air, with PCE improving ~14% after 200 hours due to fill factor improvement and remaining stable thereafter

nodes.label: liquid_cell_degradation
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3 perovskite nanoparticles are unstable in iodide-containing liquid electrolyte due to rapid dissolution, while solid-state architecture dramatically improves device stability

nodes.label: electron_transfer_question
nodes.content: Why is no significant electron injection from excited CH3NH3PbI3 into TiO2 observed on the sub-ns timescale despite favorable band alignment?

nodes.label: perovskite_materials
nodes.content: Organolead halide perovskite compounds CH3NH3PbBr3 (cubic, a=5.9 A) and CH3NH3PbI3 (tetragonal, a=8.855 A, c=12.659 A) synthesized from abundant sources Pb, C, N, and halogen, deposited from precursor solutions via spin-coating

nodes.label: pce_3_8_pct
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3-sensitized photoelectrochemical cell achieves power conversion efficiency of 3.81% under AM 1.5 simulated sunlight at 100 mW/cm2, with Jsc=11.0 mA/cm2, Voc=0.61 V, and fill factor 0.57

nodes.label: high_voc_bromide
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbBr3-sensitized cell yields a high open-circuit voltage of 0.96 V with Jsc=5.57 mA/cm2, fill factor 0.59, and PCE of 3.13%

nodes.label: perovskite_sensitizes_tio2
nodes.content: Organolead halide perovskite nanocrystalline particles efficiently sensitize mesoporous TiO2 for visible-light conversion, confirmed by anodic photocurrent generation and IPCE measurements

nodes.label: ipce_bromide_65_pct
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbBr3/TiO2 cell achieves maximum IPCE of 65% in the visible region (lambda < 600 nm) with sharp band-gap absorption onset near 570 nm

nodes.label: ipce_iodide_extended
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI3/TiO2 cell shows extended spectral response to 800 nm but lower peak IPCE of 45%, reflecting broader but less intense absorption

nodes.label: bandgap_bathochromic_shift
nodes.content: Halogen substitution from Br to I in CH3NH3PbX3 causes a bathochromic shift in absorption, analogous to silver halide ionic crystals

nodes.label: valence_band_levels
nodes.content: Valence-band levels of CH3NH3PbBr3 and CH3NH3PbI3 are at ~5.38 and 5.44 eV versus vacuum level, respectively, as measured by photoelectron spectroscopy

nodes.label: conduction_band_levels
nodes.content: Conduction-band levels of CH3NH3PbBr3 and CH3NH3PbI3 are at ~3.36 and 4.0 eV respectively, allowing electron injection into the TiO2 conduction band (~4.0 eV)

nodes.label: high_voc_origin
nodes.content: The high Voc of 0.96 V in CH3NH3PbBr3 cells is due to the more positive electrochemical potential of the Br2/Br- redox couple compared to I2/I-, expanding the range of photovoltage

nodes.label: exceeds_quantum_dots
nodes.content: The 3.81% PCE of the CH3NH3PbI3 cell is significantly higher than efficiencies obtained to date with non-organic sensitizers and quantum dots (CdS, CdSe, PbS, InP, InAs)

nodes.label: perovskite_series_potential
nodes.content: A series of organic-inorganic perovskite materials CH3NH3MX3 (M=Pb, Sn; X=halogen) with different energy gaps are targets for optimizing photovoltaic cell performance

nodes.label: durability_question
nodes.content: How can the photocurrent decay observed under continuous irradiation for open cells exposed to air be mitigated to improve perovskite photovoltaic cell lifetime?

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Meso-superstructured solar cell (MSSC) using mixed-halide perovskite CH3NH3PbI2Cl processed from DMF precursor via spin-coating in ambient conditions, with either mesoporous TiO2 or insulating Al2O3 scaffold, spiro-OMeTAD hole conductor, and Ag electrode, measured under AM 1.5 100 mW/cm2 simulated sunlight

nodes.label: mixed_halide_perovskite
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI2Cl mixed-halide perovskite with tetragonal structure (a=8.825 A, b=8.835 A, c=11.24 A), long-range crystalline domains >200 nm, and remarkable stability to processing in air, unlike CH3NH3PbI3

nodes.label: pce_10_9_pct
nodes.content: Al2O3-based meso-superstructured solar cell achieves PCE of 10.9% with Jsc=17.8 mA/cm2, Voc=0.98 V, and fill factor 0.63, the highest reported for perovskite or hybrid solar cells at the time

nodes.label: voc_above_1_1v
nodes.content: An Al2O3-based device achieves Voc above 1.13 V with Jsc=15.4 mA/cm2 and fill factor 0.45, yielding 7.8% PCE, demonstrating exceptionally high photovoltage generation

nodes.label: tio2_cell_7_6_pct
nodes.content: Perovskite-sensitized TiO2 solar cell achieves PCE of 7.6% with Jsc=17.8 mA/cm2, Voc=0.80 V, and fill factor 0.53

nodes.label: al2o3_increases_voc
nodes.content: Replacing mesoporous TiO2 with insulating Al2O3 increases Voc by >200 mV while maintaining comparable Jsc, because electrons are confined in the perovskite rather than injected into the metal oxide

nodes.label: perovskite_n_type_transport
nodes.content: The perovskite layer functions as both absorber and n-type semiconductor, transporting electrons through the film thickness, as demonstrated by charge collection faster by a factor >10 in Al2O3 cells vs TiO2 cells

nodes.label: chemical_capacitance_explanation
nodes.content: The increased Voc in Al2O3 cells is caused by substantial reduction of chemical capacitance: without mesoporous TiO2 sub-bandgap states, the quasi-Fermi level for electrons moves nearer to the conduction band for the same charge density

nodes.label: low_energy_losses
nodes.content: The difference between optical bandgap (1.55 eV) and Voc (1.1 V) is only 0.45 eV, competitive with the best thin-film photovoltaic technologies and far superior to typical 0.7-0.8 eV losses in dye-sensitized and organic solar cells

nodes.label: absorption_stability
nodes.content: CH3NH3PbI2Cl perovskite film maintains absorbance ~1.8 at 500 nm (98.4% absorption) over 1000 hours of constant simulated full sunlight illumination without UV filtration

nodes.label: hole_transfer_effective
nodes.content: Hole transfer from photoexcited perovskite to spiro-OMeTAD is highly effective in both TiO2 and Al2O3 architectures, confirmed by PIA showing oxidized spiro-OMeTAD features at 525, 750, and 1200 nm

nodes.label: planar_junction_feasible
nodes.content: A planar-junction diode (FTO/TiO2/CH3NH3PbI2Cl/spiro-OMeTAD/Ag) with ~150 nm perovskite film achieves Jsc=7.13 mA/cm2, Voc=0.64 V, FF=0.4, and PCE=1.8%, confirming perovskite can function without mesoporous scaffold

nodes.label: perovskite_conductivity
nodes.content: The perovskite absorber has conductivity on the order of 10^-3 S/cm, while spiro-OMeTAD is less conductive (~10^-5 S/cm), requiring careful balance between shunt and series resistance

nodes.label: excitonic_vs_pn
nodes.content: Is the MSSC fundamentally excitonic or a distributed p-n junction? Do free charges generate in the bulk or are highly mobile excitons quenched at the perovskite-spiro-OMeTAD interface?

nodes.content: FA-dominated perovskite (FA0.93Cs0.07PbI3) with antisolvent-free deposition. Module structure: glass/FTO/SnO2/perovskite/passivator/spiro-OMeTAD/Au. Small devices (0.14 cm2), large devices (1.04 cm2), mini-modules (13.44 cm2), and fully slot-die printed large modules (310 cm2 and 802 cm2).

nodes.label: phase_problem
nodes.content: Phase separation occurs in double-halide alloyed 2D perovskites with long-chain (>10) alkylamine ligands, producing uneven n-value distribution on 3D perovskite surfaces. This energy disorder deteriorates interfacial charge transport in large-area modules.

nodes.label: phase_separation_discovery
nodes.title: phase_separation_discovery
nodes.content: Long-chain alkylamine ligands (>10 carbons) cause phase separation in double-halide alloyed 2D perovskites, while triple-halide compositions successfully eliminate the problematic phase separation

nodes.label: homogeneous_2d_passivation
nodes.title: homogeneous_2d_passivation
nodes.content: Incorporating formamidinium bromide (FABr) into n-dodecylammonium halide (DAX) post-treatment enables growth of phase-pure n=2 2D perovskite capping layer with homogeneous morphology on 3D perovskite surfaces

nodes.label: small_cell_2561
nodes.title: small_cell_25_61_percent
nodes.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 25.61% for 0.14 cm2 small devices using the DABr/FABr homogeneous 2D passivation strategy with antisolvent-free processing

nodes.label: large_cell_2462
nodes.title: large_cell_24_62_percent
nodes.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 24.62% for 1.04 cm2 large devices, demonstrating less than 5% efficiency loss per ten times magnification

nodes.label: mini_module_2360
nodes.title: mini_module_23_60_percent
nodes.content: Champion active-area efficiency of 23.60% for 13.44 cm2 mini-modules, maintaining the small efficiency loss scaling trend

nodes.label: printed_module_310
nodes.title: printed_module_310_cm2
nodes.content: Fully slot-die printed large modules achieve champion aperture-area efficiency of 18.90% for 20 cm x 20 cm modules (310 cm2 aperture area)

nodes.label: printed_module_802
nodes.title: printed_module_802_cm2
nodes.content: Fully slot-die printed modules achieve champion aperture-area efficiency of 17.59% for 30 cm x 30 cm modules (802 cm2 aperture area), demonstrating the feasibility of upscaling manufacturing

nodes.label: module_mppt_stability
nodes.title: module_mppt_t80_2000h
nodes.content: Encapsulated mini-modules exhibit remarkable operational stability with T80 lifetime exceeding 2000 hours at MPPT under continuous light illumination

nodes.label: triple_halide_mechanism
nodes.title: triple_halide_mechanism
nodes.content: Triple-halide compositions (I/Br/Cl) have lower formation enthalpy than double-halide alloys for n=2 2D perovskite, enabling preferential formation of phase-pure n=2 structure confirmed by in situ PL and GIWAXS

nodes.label: fabr_accelerated_growth
nodes.title: fabr_accelerated_growth
nodes.content: FABr addition accelerates the formation of n=2 2D perovskite, appearing at 33 seconds during spin coating versus 40 seconds for DABr alone, enabling homogeneous coverage even at room temperature

nodes.label: universal_ligand
nodes.title: universal_ligand_strategy
nodes.content: The FABr-assisted homogeneous passivation strategy is universal, successfully suppressing phase separation for most conventional 2D ligands including BA, OA, DA, PMA, PEA, and NMA

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Inverted (p-i-n) perovskite solar cells with glass/ITO/2PACz/3D perovskite/2D perovskite/C60/BCP/Ag architecture, using oleylammonium iodide (OLAI) post-treatment to form 2D perovskite passivation layers on the 3D perovskite surface at the electron-selective contact, encapsulated with vacuum-laminated glass/encapsulant and butyl rubber edge sealing for stability testing

nodes.label: olai_passivation
nodes.content: Oleylammonium iodide (OLAI) molecules applied as post-treatment on 3D perovskite surfaces form Ruddlesden-Popper-phase 2D perovskite layers; the dimensionality (n) is controlled by annealing conditions: thermal annealing (2D-TA) produces n=1 dominated layers, while room-temperature processing (2D-RT) yields mixed n=1 and n=2 layers with more pronounced n=2 character

nodes.label: iec_61215_standard
nodes.content: IEC 61215:2016 industrial standard requires photovoltaic modules to pass damp-heat testing at 85C and 85% relative humidity for >1000 hours with <5% relative loss in PCE, representing a critical benchmark for commercial viability

nodes.label: pce_24_3_pct
nodes.content: 2D-RT passivated inverted PSCs achieve maximum PCE of 24.3% with stabilized PCE of ~24%, Voc=1.20 V, and FF=~82%, representing ~2% absolute PCE gain over unpassivated control devices

nodes.label: energy_loss_0_34_ev
nodes.content: 2D-RT passivation minimizes device energy loss (Eg - qVoc) to 0.34 eV, representing ~96% of the thermodynamic Voc limit (1.262 V) for a bandgap of 1.55 eV, comparable to state-of-the-art GaAs solar cells at 98%

nodes.label: damp_heat_t95_over_1000h
nodes.content: 2D-RT passivated encapsulated devices retain >95% of initial PCE (T95) after >1200 hours at damp-heat conditions (85C/85%RH), passing the IEC 61215:2016 industrial stability standard

nodes.label: post_dh_pce_19_3_pct
nodes.content: After >1000 hours of damp-heat testing, three devices show average PCE of 19.3+/-0.69%, representing a very high retained PCE compared to prior reports

nodes.label: room_temp_higher_dimensionality
nodes.content: Room-temperature OLAI processing (2D-RT) forms higher-dimensionality 2D perovskite layers (n=1 and n=2 mixed, with more pronounced n=2) while thermal annealing (2D-TA) produces only n=1 layers; higher-n layers have lower formation energy and better electronic alignment with C60

nodes.label: cbm_alignment_with_c60
nodes.content: The conduction band minimum (CBM) of 2D-RT films is closer to the CBM of C60 at the n-type contact, enabling more efficient charge transfer at the 2D/3D perovskite interface; 2D-TA films have higher CBM with less efficient charge transfer

nodes.label: reduced_trap_recombination
nodes.content: 2D-passivated devices exhibit longer charge recombination lifetime and lower ideality factor than control devices, confirming reduced trap-assisted recombination at 3D/C60 interfaces

nodes.label: universal_passivation
nodes.content: The OLAI 2D-RT passivation approach is universal across various perovskite compositions (various bandgaps) and deposition techniques (one-step, two-step, blade-coating), yielding systematic absolute PCE enhancement of 1.5-2.0%

nodes.label: mppt_95_pct_500h
nodes.content: Encapsulated 2D-RT devices retain up to ~95% of initial PCE after >500 hours of maximum power point tracking under simulated 1-sun illumination in ambient air, while control devices retain <90% for only ~100 hours

nodes.label: structural_robustness_500h_85c
nodes.content: No substantial change in structural and optical properties of 2D perovskite passivation films (both 3D and 2D perovskites) after >500 hours of thermal annealing at 85C under dark conditions, confirming robustness of the 2D passivation approach

nodes.label: reproducibility_high
nodes.content: The 2D-RT passivation approach shows less than 0.5% deviation for person-to-person variations among seven different researchers, confirming high reproducibility

nodes.label: c60_weak_bonding_problem
nodes.content: C60 is only weakly bonded to perovskite layers, inducing high energetic disorder between perovskite and C60 that limits device performance at elevated operating temperatures, and a thin C60 layer is insufficient to protect against moisture or oxygen ingress

nodes.label: scale_up_2d_rt
nodes.content: Can the 2D-RT passivation approach be scaled to module-level fabrication using slot-die or blade-coating processes, and does the room-temperature processing advantage translate to industrial throughput?

nodes.label: long_term_outdoor_stability
nodes.content: How do 2D-RT passivated devices perform under real outdoor conditions with daily thermal cycling, UV exposure, and varying humidity beyond the 1000-hour damp-heat benchmark?

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with WBG (~1.78 eV) FA0.8Cs0.2Pb(I0.62Br0.38)3 top cell and NBG (~1.25 eV) Pb-Sn perovskite bottom cell. Device area 0.049 cm2 (small) and 1.05 cm2 (large). SAM-modified NiO HTL for WBG subcell. Encapsulated devices tested under simulated AM 1.5G at ambient conditions.

nodes.label: nbg_challenge
nodes.content: Mixed Pb-Sn narrow-bandgap perovskite subcells constrain all-perovskite tandem performance due to high trap density at the film surface. Conventional 2D/3D heterojunctions reduce surface recombination but induce transport losses that limit device fill factors.

nodes.label: phj_concept
nodes.title: phj_3d_3d_bilayer_concept
nodes.content: An immiscible 3D/3D bilayer perovskite heterojunction (PHJ) with type-II band structure at the Pb-Sn perovskite/ETL interface can simultaneously suppress interfacial non-radiative recombination and facilitate charge extraction without transport losses from 2D interlayers

nodes.label: nbg_pce_238
nodes.title: nbg_pce_23_8_percent
nodes.content: The 3D/3D bilayer PHJ enables Pb-Sn PSCs with 1.2 um absorber to reach 23.8% PCE with Voc of 0.873 V and FF of 82.6%, substantially higher than the 21.0% control

nodes.label: tandem_pce_285
nodes.title: tandem_pce_28_5_percent
nodes.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with PHJ in the NBG subcell achieve a record PCE of 28.5% (certified stabilized 28.0% by JET) with Voc of 2.112 V, Jsc of 16.5 mA cm-2, and FF of 81.9%

nodes.label: hybrid_deposition
nodes.title: hybrid_deposition_phj
nodes.content: A hybrid evaporation-solution processing method enables non-destructive deposition of a 50 nm Pb-halide wide-bandgap perovskite (FA0.7Cs0.3Pb(I0.85Br0.14)3) on top of the Pb-Sn NBG perovskite, forming a clearly defined 3D/3D bilayer heterostructure with limited metal-ion intermixing

nodes.label: type_ii_band
nodes.title: type_ii_band_alignment
nodes.content: The PHJ creates type-II band alignment between Pb-Sn NBG and FL-WBG perovskites that reduces hole concentration near the defective interface layer and facilitates electron extraction into C60, confirmed by ultrafast TA spectroscopy showing charge transfer from Pb-Sn to FL-WBG

nodes.label: elqy_improvement
nodes.title: elqy_improvement_phj
nodes.content: PHJ devices show electroluminescence quantum yield of 3.09% versus 0.47% for controls at 1-sun current density, corresponding to Voc loss reduction from 147 mV to 97 mV

nodes.label: phj_structural_stability
nodes.title: phj_structural_stability
nodes.content: The 3D/3D PHJ retains its distinct heterostructure with no evidence of Sn diffusion into the FL-WBG layer after 60 days of storage in N2 glovebox, confirmed by EDX and ToF-SIMS

nodes.label: tandem_operational_stability
nodes.title: tandem_operational_stability_600h
nodes.content: Encapsulated tandem devices with PHJ retain over 90% of initial performance after 600 hours of continuous MPP tracking under simulated 1-sun illumination

nodes.label: large_area_tandem_269
nodes.title: large_area_tandem_26_9_percent
nodes.content: Large-area all-perovskite tandem devices (1.05 cm2) achieve 26.9% PCE with Voc of 2.149 V, Jsc of 15.7 mA cm-2, and FF of 79.8%

nodes.label: voc_ff_improvement
nodes.title: voc_ff_improvement_phj
nodes.content: PHJ in the NBG subcell improves average Voc by 45 mV (0.824 to 0.869 V) and FF by 2.3% (78.5% to 80.8%) compared with controls, while Jsc remains similar

nodes.label: tandem_ff_improvement
nodes.title: tandem_ff_improvement_phj
nodes.content: PHJ tandems show substantially improved FF from 78.0% to 81.4% and PCE from 26.0% to 27.7% compared with control tandems, because the tandem FF is limited by the subpar NBG subcell under current matching

nodes.label: beyond_30_outlook
nodes.title: beyond_30_percent_outlook
nodes.content: Combining optical and electrical loss reductions, an all-perovskite tandem PCE higher than 30% is achievable by empirically assuming Voc of 2.2 V, Jsc of 17 mA cm-2, and FF of 82%

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Planar heterojunction p-i-n solar cell with dual-source vapour-deposited CH3NH3PbI3-xClx perovskite: CH3NH3I and PbCl2 evaporated simultaneously at 4:1 molar ratio under 10^-5 mbar onto compact TiO2/FTO substrate, annealed at 100C for 45 min, followed by solution-processed spiro-OMeTAD and evaporated Ag electrode, measured under AM 1.5 at 101 mW/cm2

nodes.label: vapour_deposition_method
nodes.content: Dual-source thermal evaporation: CH3NH3I at ~116C (5.3 A/s) and PbCl2 at ~320C (1 A/s) for ~128 min, producing ~330 nm thick perovskite film after annealing. Substrate holder rotated and water-cooled to ~21C for uniform coating

nodes.label: pce_15_4_pct
nodes.content: Best vapour-deposited planar heterojunction perovskite cell achieves PCE of 15.4% with Jsc=21.5 mA/cm2, Voc=1.07 V, and fill factor 0.67, measured under AM 1.5 simulated sunlight at 101 mW/cm2

nodes.label: batch_average_12_3
nodes.content: Average performance of 12 identically processed vapour-deposited cells is PCE=12.3% +/- 2.0%, Jsc=18.9 +/- 1.8 mA/cm2, Voc=1.05 +/- 0.03 V, FF=0.62 +/- 0.05

nodes.label: nanostructure_not_necessary
nodes.content: Nanostructuring (mesoporous scaffold) is not necessary to achieve high efficiency with perovskite absorbers; a simple planar heterojunction thin-film architecture can deliver over 15% PCE

nodes.label: vapour_film_uniformity
nodes.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite films are extremely uniform with crystalline features on the scale of hundreds of nanometres, in contrast to solution-processed films with partial coverage and crystalline platelets tens of micrometres in size

nodes.label: solution_planar_8_6_pct
nodes.content: Solution-processed planar heterojunction perovskite cell achieves 8.6% PCE with Jsc=17.6 mA/cm2, Voc=0.84 V, and FF=0.58, but with undulating film (50-410 nm thickness variation) and pinholes causing shunting paths

nodes.label: phase_purity
nodes.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite films show high phase purity with only minor PbI2 (12.65 degrees) and no measurable CH3NH3PbCl3 (15.68 degrees) XRD peaks, confirming well-formed mixed-halide perovskite

nodes.label: diffusion_length_lower_bound
nodes.content: The electron and hole diffusion length in CH3NH3PbI3-xClx perovskite is at least 330 nm (the film thickness), as evidenced by efficient charge collection in planar heterojunction cells

nodes.label: same_crystal_structure
nodes.content: Both vapour-deposited and solution-cast films produce the same mixed-halide perovskite with orthorhombic crystal structure, as confirmed by identical XRD peak positions at 14.12, 28.44, and 43.23 degrees

nodes.label: tandem_compatibility
nodes.content: Vapour-deposited perovskite technology is compatible with conventional silicon and CIGS processing, making it suitable as a top cell in hybrid tandem configurations

nodes.label: cl_apical_position
nodes.content: Cl atoms in the mixed-halide perovskite reside in apical positions out of the PbI4 plane rather than in equatorial octahedral sites, consistent with a slight contraction of the c-axis compared to CH3NH3PbI3

nodes.label: diffusion_length_precise
nodes.content: What are the precise electron and hole diffusion lengths in vapour-deposited CH3NH3PbI3-xClx, and what are the primary excitation and free-charge generation mechanisms?

nodes.label: manufacturing_route
nodes.content: Will vapour deposition emerge as the preferred route for perovskite solar cell manufacture, or will solution processing eventually match its film quality?
not_both_true(A, B)

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Inverted (p-i-n) PSCs with FTO/NiOx/Me-4PACz/perovskite/passivation/C60/BCP/Ag architecture, using normal bandgap (~1.5 eV) perovskite. Active areas of 0.05 cm2 and 1.5 cm2 tested. Thermal stability tested under ISOS-D-2 (85 C in N2) and operational stability under ISOS-L-3 (1 sun, 65 C, ambient air).

nodes.label: pin_gap_setting
nodes.content: Inverted p-i-n PSCs have potential stability and tandem-compatibility advantages over regular n-i-p, but their PCEs rarely surpass 24% under quasi-steady-state (QSS) protocol due to higher recombination at the perovskite/C60 interface.

nodes.label: dmdp_strategy
nodes.title: dmdp_bimolecular_passivation
nodes.content: A dual-molecule passivation strategy combining diammonium (PDAI2) for field-effect passivation with sulfur-modified methylthio (3MTPAI) for chemical passivation simultaneously suppresses both interface and surface recombination at the perovskite/C60 top interface

nodes.label: certified_pce_251
nodes.title: certified_qss_pce_25_1_percent
nodes.content: The DMDP strategy achieves a certified quasi-steady-state PCE of 25.1% for inverted PSCs (NREL certification, 0.05 cm2), the first inverted PSC to exceed 25% under QSS protocol

nodes.label: champion_pce_264
nodes.title: champion_pce_26_4_percent
nodes.content: The champion DMDP device achieves a PCE of 26.4% with Jsc of 26.2 mA cm-2, Voc of 1.17 V, and FF of 85.8%

nodes.label: carrier_lifetime_5x
nodes.title: carrier_lifetime_5x_improvement
nodes.content: DMDP passivation leads to a fivefold longer carrier lifetime and one-third the photoluminescence quantum yield loss compared to control

nodes.label: operational_stability_2000h
nodes.title: operational_stability_2000h
nodes.content: Encapsulated DMDP devices maintain 96% of initial PCE after 2000 hours of continuous 1-sun operation at 65 C in ambient air (ISOS-L-3 protocol)

nodes.label: thermal_stability
nodes.title: thermal_stability_95_percent_1600h
nodes.content: DMDP devices retain 95% of initial PCE after 1600 hours of thermal aging at 85 C in nitrogen (ISOS-D-2 protocol)

nodes.label: methylthio_binding
nodes.title: methylthio_stronger_binding
nodes.content: The sulfur-modified methylthio molecule (3MTPA) has stronger binding energy to iodide vacancy defects on the perovskite surface compared to conventional alkyl ammonium ligands, driven by S-Pb coordination bonding and enhanced hydrogen bonding with formamidinium

nodes.label: field_effect_passivation
nodes.title: field_effect_passivation_diammonium
nodes.content: Diammonium ligands (PDAI2) induce n-type doping and a surface dipole that repels minority carriers (holes) from the perovskite/C60 interface, achieving field-effect passivation that reduces contact-induced interface recombination

nodes.label: dark_current_reduction
nodes.title: dark_current_2_orders_reduction
nodes.content: DMDP-treated devices show a dark saturation current (J0) reduction by two orders of magnitude compared with control devices

nodes.label: tandem_281
nodes.title: all_perovskite_tandem_28_1_percent
nodes.content: The DMDP strategy is applicable to monolithic all-perovskite tandem solar cells, achieving 28.1% PCE with Voc of 2.14 V and stabilized PCE of 27.1% under MPPT

nodes.label: universal_bandgap
nodes.title: dmdp_universal_bandgap
nodes.content: The DMDP strategy improves PCE by 13-14% for both narrow-bandgap (~1.2 eV) and wide-bandgap (~1.8 eV) perovskite compositions, demonstrating universality across perovskite bandgaps

nodes.label: large_area_24
nodes.title: large_area_24_percent
nodes.content: DMDP-treated devices with 1.5 cm2 area deliver a PCE of 24.0%, demonstrating scalability of the passivation approach

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with WBG (~1.78 eV) FA0.8Cs0.2Pb(I0.62Br0.38)3 top cell and NBG (~1.25 eV) Pb-Sn perovskite bottom cell. Device areas 0.049 cm2 and 1.05 cm2. Certified by JET (Japan Electrical Safety and Environment Technology Laboratories). PEDOT:PSS HTL with sulfanilic acid (SA) dipolar passivation.

nodes.label: buried_interface_problem
nodes.content: Deep-level traps at the buried perovskite/HTL interface in mixed Pb-Sn NBG subcells cause severe non-radiative recombination. Conventional long-chain amine passivation improves Voc but impairs charge transport, reducing Jsc and FF due to insulating barriers.

nodes.label: dipolar_passivation_strategy
nodes.title: dipolar_passivation_strategy
nodes.content: A dipolar-passivation strategy using sulfanilic acid (SA) at the HTL/Pb-Sn perovskite interface reduces trap density while enabling precise energy-level alignment through oriented dipoles, simultaneously suppressing recombination and enhancing carrier extraction

nodes.label: nbg_pce_249
nodes.title: nbg_pce_24_9_percent
nodes.content: Dipolar-passivated Pb-Sn PSCs achieve 24.9% PCE with Voc of 0.911 V, Jsc of 33.1 mA cm-2, and FF of 82.6%, representing one of the highest reported PCEs for mixed Pb-Sn PSCs

nodes.label: tandem_pce_306
nodes.title: tandem_pce_30_6_percent
nodes.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells with dipolar passivation achieve 30.6% PCE (certified stabilized 30.1% by JET) for 0.049 cm2 area

nodes.label: large_tandem_296
nodes.title: large_tandem_29_6_percent
nodes.content: Large-area all-perovskite tandem (1.05 cm2) achieves certified stabilized PCE of 29.6%, demonstrating scalability

nodes.label: diffusion_length_62um
nodes.title: diffusion_length_6_2_um
nodes.content: Dipolar passivation extends carrier diffusion length to 6.2 um (versus 4.8 um for control) with mobility of 113.5 cm2 V-1 s-1 (versus 67.5 cm2 V-1 s-1), as measured by terahertz spectroscopy

nodes.label: ohmic_contact
nodes.title: ohmic_contact_dipolar
nodes.content: The oriented SA dipoles create type-II energy-level alignment with ohmic contact at the HTL/Pb-Sn perovskite interface, facilitating efficient hole injection into PEDOT:PSS while repelling electrons from the interface

nodes.label: elqy_705
nodes.title: elqy_7_05_percent
nodes.content: Dipolar-passivation devices show electroluminescence quantum yield of 7.05% versus 2.40% for controls at 1-sun current density, corresponding to Voc loss reduction from 103 mV to 73 mV

nodes.label: dark_storage_1000h
nodes.title: dark_storage_1000h
nodes.content: Dipolar-passivated devices show no significant PCE degradation after over 1000 hours in a nitrogen glovebox without encapsulation

nodes.label: sa_molecular_orientation
nodes.title: sa_molecular_orientation
nodes.content: SA molecules adopt a preferred orientation with -NH3+ anchoring to the perovskite bottom surface and -SO3- directed towards PEDOT:PSS, confirmed by AIMD simulations and KPFM measurements showing surface potential decrease from -80 mV to -162 mV

nodes.label: tandem_voc_50mv_gain
nodes.title: tandem_voc_50mv_gain
nodes.content: Dipolar-passivation tandem devices show average Voc 50 mV higher than controls, with average PCE of 30.3 +/- 0.3% versus 29.1 +/- 0.2%

nodes.label: wbg_subcell_205
nodes.title: wbg_subcell_20_5_percent
nodes.content: The wide-bandgap subcell achieves 20.5% PCE with Voc of 1.329 V, Jsc of 18.4 mA cm-2, and FF of 83.8%, using SAM-modified NiO HTL

nodes.label: qfls_improvement
nodes.title: qfls_improvement_dipolar
nodes.content: Dipolar passivation improves QFLS from 904 meV to 940 meV at the HTL/perovskite interface (full device stack), with the primary QFLS loss reduction attributed to enhanced HTL interface quality

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: FAPbI3 perovskite solar cells on mesoporous TiO2/FTO substrates using anti-solvent method with methylenediammonium dichloride (MDACl2) added to the FAPbI3 precursor at 1.9-5.7 mol%, with CuPC (copper phthalocyanine) or spiro-OMeTAD as hole transport material, Au electrode, measured under AM 1.5G at 100 mW/cm2

nodes.label: mdac12_dopant
nodes.content: Methylenediammonium dichloride (MDACl2) is a divalent diammonium dopant with ionic radius (262 pm) comparable to FA+ (256 pm); MDA2+ can form more hydrogen bonds with I- than FA+ or MA+ due to having more H atoms, potentially stabilizing the alpha-FAPbI3 phase in smaller amounts than MA

nodes.label: certified_23_7_pct
nodes.content: FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 PSC achieves certified stabilized PCE of 23.73% via quasi-steady-state method at Newport, with Jsc=26.1 mA/cm2, Voc=1.15 V, FF=79.0%, the highest reported for mp-TiO2-based PSCs

nodes.label: record_jsc_26_7
nodes.content: A second certified device achieves Jsc=26.70 mA/cm2 (highest reported for FAPbI3-based PSCs) with Voc=1.144 V and FF=77.56%, corresponding to stabilized PCE of 23.69%

nodes.label: uncertified_24_66_pct
nodes.content: The best-performing lab-measured device achieves PCE of 24.66% under reverse scan with Jsc=26.50 mA/cm2, Voc=1.14 V, and FF=81.77%

nodes.label: alpha_phase_stabilization
nodes.content: Adding 3.8-5.7 mol% MDACl2 to FAPbI3 stabilizes the alpha-phase against transition to the delta-phase even after 24 hours at 80% humidity, whereas pure FAPbI3 and FAPbI3 with only 1.9 mol% MDACl2 completely convert to the delta-phase

nodes.label: inherent_bandgap_preserved
nodes.content: MDACl2 addition preserves the inherent narrow bandgap of FAPbI3: PL emission shifts minimally from 826 nm (pristine) to 822 nm (3.8 mol%), compared to 816 nm for the MAPbBr3-stabilized control, enabling higher photocurrent through broader absorption

nodes.label: mida_hbond_mechanism
nodes.content: MDA2+ stabilizes alpha-FAPbI3 through two mechanisms: (1) stronger hydrogen bonding with I- due to more H atoms than FA+ or MA+, and (2) stronger ionic interaction from its divalent charge state, enabling stabilization in smaller amounts than MA

nodes.label: cl_residue_at_interface
nodes.content: XPS and TOF-SIMS show higher residual Cl- content in FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 than in the MAPbBr3 control, concentrated at the TiO2/perovskite interface, which is expected to enhance light stability of the PSC

nodes.label: defect_density_low
nodes.content: Space-charge-limited current measurements show electron trap densities of 5.7x10^15 cm-3 for FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2, lower than 1.0x10^16 cm-3 for the MAPbBr3 control, indicating that FA vacancy defects do not act as deep electron traps

nodes.label: carrier_lifetime_1562_ns
nodes.content: FAPbI3:3.8 mol% MDACl2 films exhibit nonradiative recombination lifetime of 1562 ns on quartz, more than double the control value of 715 ns, indicating reduced trap-assisted recombination

nodes.label: humidity_stability_85rh
nodes.content: Unencapsulated target device retains >90% of initial PCE after 70 hours at 85% RH and 25C, while the MAPbBr3-stabilized control degrades to 40% of initial PCE

nodes.label: thermal_stability_150c
nodes.content: Unencapsulated target device retains >90% of initial PCE after 20 hours annealing at 150C in air (~25% RH), while the control degrades to <20% of initial PCE due to MA evaporation from the MAPbBr3 component

nodes.label: photostability_600h_mppt
nodes.content: Encapsulated target device with spiro-OMeTAD HTM maintains ~90% of initial PCE (>23.0%) over 600 hours of maximum power point tracking under full solar illumination (AM 1.5G, 100 mW/cm2) in ambient conditions without UV filter

nodes.label: optimal_mda_concentration
nodes.content: What is the optimal MDACl2 concentration window for balancing alpha-phase stability against defect formation from FA vacancies, and does the MDACl2 approach transfer to mixed-halide or mixed-cation perovskite compositions?

nodes.label: long_term_operational_stability
nodes.content: Can MDACl2-stabilized FAPbI3 devices maintain >90% PCE beyond 1000 hours of continuous operation under damp-heat conditions (85C/85% RH), which is the industry standard IEC 61215 test?

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: Monolithic all-perovskite tandem solar cells combining a wide-bandgap (~1.8 eV) mixed Br/I perovskite front cell with a narrow-bandgap (~1.2 eV) mixed Pb-Sn perovskite back cell, using CF3-PA (4-trifluoromethyl-phenylammonium) additive in the Pb-Sn precursor solution at 0.3 mol%

nodes.label: cf3_pa_passivator
nodes.content: 4-trifluoromethyl-phenylammonium (CF3-PA) is an ammonium cation passivator where the highly electronegative fluorine atoms withdraw electron density from the aromatic ring, leaving higher electropositivity at the NH3+ side and enhancing binding with negatively charged defect sites on Pb-Sn perovskite surfaces

nodes.label: tandem_26_4_certified
nodes.content: All-perovskite tandem solar cells achieve certified stabilized PCE of 26.4% (JET calibration), exceeding the best single-junction perovskite solar cell efficiency and comparable to the best single-crystalline silicon cells

nodes.label: tandem_26_7_best
nodes.content: The best tandem cell achieves PCE of 26.7% from reverse scan with Voc=2.03 V, Jsc=16.5 mA/cm2, and FF=79.9%, with stabilized PCE of 26.6%

nodes.label: cf3_pa_best_passivator
nodes.content: Among three passivators tested (PEA, PA, CF3-PA), CF3-PA produces the best Pb-Sn PSC performance across all PV parameters (Voc, Jsc, FF, PCE) with 1.2 um thick absorber layers

nodes.label: diffusion_length_5_um
nodes.content: CF3-PA passivation increases the carrier diffusion length in Pb-Sn perovskite films threefold from 1.8 um to 5.4 um, enabling 1.2 um thick absorber layers for high photocurrent density

nodes.label: pb_sn_psc_22_pct
nodes.content: CF3-PA passivated single-junction Pb-Sn PSCs with 1.2 um absorber achieve best PCE of 22.2% (stabilized 22.0%), with Voc=0.841 V, Jsc=33.0 mA/cm2, FF=80%, and average PCE of 20.8+/-0.5% across 237 devices

nodes.label: complete_surface_coverage
nodes.content: Ab initio molecular dynamics at 400 K show all 16 CF3-PA cations adsorb completely on the perovskite surface, compared to 15/16 for PA and 13/16 for PEA, indicating near-complete defect coverage during film formation

nodes.label: suppressed_iodine_vacancies
nodes.content: CF3-PA not only increases adsorption probability on perovskite surfaces but also suppresses iodide ion desorption at 400 K, reducing formation of both iodine vacancies and iodine interstitial defects

nodes.label: strongest_binding_energy
nodes.content: DFT calculations show CF3-PA has the strongest binding energy with acceptor-type defects (FA vacancy, Sn vacancy, I_Sn, I_Pb antisites) on Pb-Sn perovskite surfaces among all three passivators, and eliminates deep in-gap states from antisite defects

nodes.label: carrier_lifetime_966_ns
nodes.content: CF3-PA passivated Pb-Sn perovskite films exhibit effective carrier lifetime of 966 ns, compared to 437 ns (PA), 365 ns (PEA), and 159 ns (control), as measured by time-resolved photoluminescence

nodes.label: no_2d_phase_formation
nodes.content: XRD patterns show that adding trace amounts of CF3-PA does not form 2D perovskite phases even at 20 mol% concentration, maintaining pure 3D perovskite structure beneficial for charge transport through thick absorber layers

nodes.label: sn_oxidation_suppressed
nodes.content: Angle-dependent XPS measurements confirm that CF3-PA passivation successfully suppresses surface Sn2+ oxidation in Pb-Sn perovskite films by passivating undercoordinated Sn atoms and Sn vacancies

nodes.label: large_area_25_3_pct
nodes.content: Large-area tandem device (aperture area 1.05 cm2) achieves PCE of 25.3% with Voc=2.03 V, Jsc=16 mA/cm2, FF=78%, demonstrating scalability

nodes.label: tandem_stability_90_pct_600h
nodes.content: Encapsulated CF3-PA tandem devices retain 90% of initial PCE after 600 h of continuous MPP tracking under 1-sun illumination in ambient air (30-50% humidity) at approximately 35C device temperature

nodes.label: thick_absorber_enables_high_jsc
nodes.content: Increasing Pb-Sn absorber thickness from 750 to 1200 nm with CF3-PA increases tandem Jsc from 15.4 to 16.5 mA/cm2 due to enhanced near-infrared light absorption in the back subcell

nodes.label: tandem_long_term
nodes.content: Can all-perovskite tandem solar cells achieve operational stability beyond 1000 hours under continuous illumination, and how does CF3-PA passivation affect long-term ion migration in the Pb-Sn subcell?

nodes.label: device_fabrication
nodes.content: Perovskite solar cells fabricated with triple cation Cs/(MA/FA) compositions in mesoporous TiO2 architecture, measured under standard AM1.5G illumination

nodes.label: triple_cation_stable
nodes.content: Adding inorganic cesium to FA/MA mixed perovskite compositions produces thermally more stable perovskites with fewer phase impurities

nodes.label: less_processing_sensitive
nodes.content: Triple cation Cs-containing perovskite compositions are less sensitive to processing conditions than binary FA/MA compositions, enabling more reproducible device fabrication

nodes.label: stabilized_output_21_1_pct
nodes.content: Triple cation perovskite solar cells achieve a stabilized power output of 21.1%, representing the highest stabilized efficiency at the time of publication

nodes.label: operational_18_pct_250h
nodes.content: Triple cation devices maintain approximately 18% efficiency after 250 hours under operational conditions, demonstrating improved stability

nodes.label: cesium_reduces_impurities
nodes.content: Cs incorporation suppresses the formation of the non-perovskite delta-phase in FAPbI3-based compositions, producing monomorphic phase-pure perovskite films

nodes.label: reproducibility_improved
nodes.content: Triple cation devices show significantly improved batch-to-batch reproducibility compared to binary FA/MA compositions, with reduced standard deviation in PCE

nodes.label: black_phase_stable
nodes.content: The triple cation composition stabilizes the black perovskite phase at room temperature without requiring high-temperature annealing above 160C

nodes.label: morphology_improved
nodes.content: Cs-containing perovskite films exhibit smoother, more uniform morphology with larger grain size compared to binary FA/MA films

nodes.label: key_for_industrialization
nodes.content: The improved reproducibility, stability, and reduced processing sensitivity of triple cation compositions are key properties for industrialization of perovskite photovoltaics

nodes.label: exp_context
nodes.content: All-inorganic CsPbI3 perovskite solar cells with FTO/TiO2/Al2O3/CsPbI3/2D Cs2PbI2Cl2/CuSCN/Cr/Au structure, with and without 2D Cs2PbI2Cl2 capping layer between the perovskite and CuSCN hole transport layer, encapsulated in N2 atmosphere for accelerated aging at 35-110C under constant 1-sun illumination at maximum power point

nodes.label: cs2pbi2cl2_capping
nodes.content: 2D Cs2PbI2Cl2 capping layer (~20 nm thick) deposited on CsPbI3 surface by treating with CsCl solution followed by thermal annealing; this fully inorganic 2D layer avoids the cation exchange problem that prevents organic 2D layers from forming on CsPbI3 due to stronger Cs+ binding compared to MA+ or FA+

nodes.label: arrhenius_accelerated_aging
nodes.content: Accelerated aging methodology using elevated temperatures (35, 59, 85, 110C) to quantify degradation rates under constant illumination at MPP, with Arrhenius temperature dependence of degradation rates yielding acceleration factors for lifetime extrapolation to standard operating conditions (1 sun, 35C)

nodes.label: capped_pce_17_4_pct
nodes.content: Capped CsPbI3 PSCs with 2D Cs2PbI2Cl2 layer achieve champion PCE of 17.4%, the highest among fully inorganic PSCs where all functional materials in the stack are inorganic, compared to 14.9% for uncapped devices

nodes.label: voc_and_ff_improvement
nodes.content: The 2D capping layer increases both Voc and FF in capped PSCs relative to uncapped devices, consistent with surface passivation reducing nonradiative recombination losses at the perovskite/HTL interface

nodes.label: trpl_lifetime_increase
nodes.content: Time-resolved photoluminescence shows the carrier lifetime increases from 14 ns (uncapped) to >62 ns (capped), indicating effective suppression of nonradiative recombination at the CsPbI3 surface and extended carrier diffusion length

nodes.label: no_degradation_at_35c
nodes.content: Capped PSCs show no noticeable PCE degradation after 3531 hours of continuous operation at 35C under constant 1-sun illumination at MPP in (65+/-26)% RH air

nodes.label: t80_2100h_at_110c
nodes.content: Capped PSCs exhibit T80 lifetime (time to 80% of initial PCE) of >2100 hours at 110C under constant 1-sun illumination, while uncapped devices degrade substantially faster at all elevated temperatures

nodes.label: extrapolated_t80_5_years
nodes.content: Using experimentally determined acceleration factor of 24.2+/-3.5 for 110C operation, the extrapolated T80 lifetime for capped devices at 35C is 51,000+/-7,000 hours (>5 years) of continuous operation under illumination

nodes.label: single_degradation_mechanism
nodes.content: Degradation rates across the entire 35-110C temperature range can be described by a single Arrhenius function, confirming the same degradation mechanism dominates at all temperatures — a critical criterion for reliable accelerated aging tests

nodes.label: activation_energy_doubled
nodes.content: The activation energy for degradation (Ea) of capped PSCs is nearly twice that of uncapped PSCs, indicating that the 2D Cs2PbI2Cl2 layer substantially stabilizes the devices against thermal degradation

nodes.label: ion_migration_suppressed
nodes.content: XRD and XPS analysis after aging at 110C shows iodine migration from CsPbI3 into CuSCN in uncapped devices (evidenced by CuSCN crystallite degradation and I 3d signal in HTL), while capped devices show no appreciable iodine migration or CuSCN structural changes

nodes.label: ion_migration_activation_doubled
nodes.content: The activation energy for ion migration (Ea,ion) in capped films is nearly twice that of uncapped films as measured by temperature-dependent conductivity, indicating the 2D cap frustrates ion migration by passivating iodine vacancies at the perovskite surface

nodes.label: universal_degradation_curve
nodes.content: When aging data from all temperatures are converted to equivalent operating time at 35C using the acceleration factor, all data collapse onto a universal degradation curve for both capped and uncapped devices, confirming a single mechanism across temperatures

nodes.label: biexponential_degradation
nodes.content: PCE degradation at all temperatures follows a biexponential function with fast and slow decay rates; the fast and slow degradation activation energies are comparable within each device type, suggesting both rates probe a single physical process (ion migration)

nodes.label: organic_psc_accelerated_aging
nodes.content: Can this Arrhenius-based accelerated aging methodology with experimentally determined acceleration factors be applied to organic-inorganic hybrid perovskite compositions such as FAPbI3 and MAPbI3, which may have additional degradation pathways from organic cation volatility?

nodes.label: ion_migration_suppression_mechanism
nodes.content: Does the 2D Cs2PbI2Cl2 capping layer suppress ion migration primarily by passivating iodine vacancies at the CsPbI3 surface, or does it also create an energetic barrier to ion transport across the perovskite/HTL interface?
